summary: Split Kirby AttackDash code out of broader Kirby/SpecialN translation units into a new matching object `melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyattackdash.c`, added a dedicated header, adjusted splits/build configuration, and updated consumers to include the narrower header. The extracted text range is 0x800F1F68-0x800F21E8 with sdata2 0x804D9368-0x804D9370, allowing this slice to be marked Matching while adjacent Kirby files remain NonMatching.
what_changed: Added new source file `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyattackdash.c` containing Kirby AttackDash and AttackDashAir routines from address range 0x800F1F68-0x800F21C0.
Added new public header `ftkirbyattackdash.h` declaring `ftKb_SpecialN_800F1F68`, `ftKb_AttackDash_*`, and `ftKb_AttackDashAir_*` entry points.
Moved `ftKb_SpecialN_800F1F68` out of `ftkirby.c` and moved AttackDash-related functions out of `ftkirbyspecialn.c`.
Removed AttackDash prototypes from broad `ftkirby.h`, leaving later SpecialN/AttackDashAir helper declarations such as `fn_800F21E8` and `ftKb_AttackDashAir_800F22D4` there.

Updated `config/GALE01/splits.txt` so `ftkirby.c` ends at 0x800F1F68, the new `ftkirbyattackdash.c` covers 0x800F1F68-0x800F21E8 plus sdata2 0x804D9368-0x804D9370, and `ftkirbyspecialn.c` starts at 0x800F21E8.
Added `Object(Matching, "melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyattackdash.c")` to `configure.py` while keeping neighboring `ftkirby.c` and `ftkirbyspecialn.c` as NonMatching.
Changed include users in `ftCo_AttackDash.c` and `itkirbycutterbeam.c` from broad `ftKirby/ftkirby.h` to dedicated `ftKirby/ftkirbyattackdash.h`.

In the new split, replaced external float constants `ftKb_Init_804D9368` and `ftKb_Init_804D936C` with direct float literals `0.0f` and `1.0f` in `ftKb_SpecialN_800F1F68`, consistent with the new sdata2 split.
Kept local transition callbacks `fn_800F1FDC` and `fn_800F20C4` as file-local static forward declarations in the new source file.
Added default no-op branches to two switch statements in `ftkirbyspecialn.c`'s `ftKb_CaSpecialN_Phys` and `ftKb_CaSpecialAirN_Phys`, likely to preserve matching after the extraction.
smart_moves: Used a focused header for AttackDash exports instead of continuing to expose unrelated Kirby declarations through `ftkirby.h`, reducing unnecessary dependencies.
Marked only the extracted `ftkirbyattackdash.c` object as Matching, avoiding overclaiming matching status for larger adjacent Kirby files.
Kept internal helper callbacks static in the new translation unit where possible, making the public ABI surface smaller.
Updated the split boundaries and build object list together, which is essential for successful link-order and section-address preservation in this project.
Moved only the address-contiguous AttackDash range, preserving the next function boundary at 0x800F21E8 for `ftkirbyspecialn.c`.
decomp_lessons: When a contiguous subrange of a large nonmatching fighter file matches, it can be split into its own object and independently marked Matching in `configure.py`.
Always update `config/GALE01/splits.txt` text and small-data boundaries when extracting a new translation unit; here both `.text` and `.sdata2` moved across Kirby file boundaries.
Prefer narrow per-feature headers such as `ftkirbyattackdash.h` over umbrella fighter headers when external users only need a small set of callbacks.
File-local transition callbacks should remain `static` after a split if they are only used inside the new source file.
Direct literals like `0.0f` and `1.0f` may be preferable to external small-data symbols after a split, but this should be validated by matching output.
Small source-level changes outside the moved block, such as adding empty `default` cases, can be part of maintaining or recovering match after translation-unit boundary changes.
naming_conventions: New Kirby feature file follows existing lowercase path/name style: `ftkirbyattackdash.c` and `ftkirbyattackdash.h` under `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/`.
Public Kirby symbols retain the `ftKb_` prefix, e.g. `ftKb_AttackDash_Anim` and `ftKb_AttackDashAir_Coll`.
Address-only or unnamed helpers retain placeholder names such as `fn_800F1FDC` and `fn_800F20C4` when no semantic name is evidenced.
Header prototypes preserve address comments like `/* 0F203C */`, matching existing project convention.
Header guard uses address-style form `GALE01_0F1FDC`, consistent with generated/address-based guards seen in this codebase.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Created a new object for the exact address interval 0x800F1F68-0x800F21E8 and registered it as `Object(Matching, ...)`.
Adjusted neighboring split endpoints so `ftkirby.c`, `ftkirbyattackdash.c`, and `ftkirbyspecialn.c` form contiguous non-overlapping ranges.
Included only the dependencies needed by the extracted functions, including `ftCo_AttackDash.h`, `ftCo_Fall.h`, `ftparts.h`, `ftanim.h`, and low-level fighter helpers.
Used wrapper functions that delegate to common AttackDash behavior (`ftCo_AttackDash_Anim`, `ftCo_AttackDash_IASA`) while preserving Kirby-specific motion-state transition wrappers.
Made callback helpers static in the new TU to constrain symbol visibility and match expected linkage where external references are not needed.
Added explicit `default: break;` cases in SpecialN physics switch statements, likely as a codegen/matching adjustment after removing the AttackDash block.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that split small matching fighter action ranges out of larger NonMatching character files.
Search for additional Kirby feature headers that replaced broad includes of `ftkirby.h`.
Compare use of address-style header guards like `GALE01_0F1FDC` in other newly split files.
Look for matching fixes involving adding explicit `default: break;` to switch statements.
Search for other PRs that adjusted `.sdata2` boundaries when splitting fighter files.
